mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and next 20-day SPX returns is non-positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2.0%, monetary policy is accommodative, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations. This positive effect on S&P 500 manifests over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 2.0%, the median 60-day SPX return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month positive and UNRATE month-over-month negative
failure_condition: When both conditions hold simultaneously, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is less than 60% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals expanding manufacturing activity, which leads to positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
regime_hint: industrial production above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: In the regime where INDPRO > its 12-month MA, the average 5-day SPX return is not statistically significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI (CPIAUCSL) increase above 3.0% signals rising inflation, which erodes real earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months following CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0%, the median 60-day SPX return is not less than zero (one-sided test p > 0.10) or Rank IC to inflation is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A higher unemployment rate (UNRATE) weakens consumer spending and corporate profits, depressing equity prices. This negative relationship holds across all regimes and predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE level and next 20-day SPX return over any rolling 5-year window is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: When both real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) grow simultaneously, it confirms broad-based demand expansion that drives corporate earnings higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter positive and RSAFS month-over-month positive
failure_condition: When both series are growing, the median 60-day SPX return is not above the unconditional median return over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal robust economic activity across construction and manufacturing, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month positive and INDPRO month-over-month positive
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the proportion of positive 5-day SPX returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.10).
